task_completion.description: Open the target pcapng file in Wireshark
task_completion.visual_evidence: Wireshark main window is open with '200722_tcp_anon.pcapng' visible in the title bar or the packet list showing loaded packets.

task_completion.description: Access the capture file properties or summary to view basic statistics
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Capture File Properties' dialog or similar statistics window is open in Wireshark, displaying metrics like total packets, time/duration, and bytes.

task_completion.description: Access I/O Graphs or similar tool to analyze the busiest 1-second interval
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Wireshark · I/O Graphs' window is open with the interval set to 1 second, showing a graph or table with a peak at the 9.0s mark.

task_completion.description: Open a text editor to create the statistics report
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, mousepad, or terminal-based editor) is open, editing a file named 'io_stats_report.txt' in the ~/Documents/captures/ directory.

task_completion.description: Record the basic statistics in the report file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows key-value pairs containing the correct basic stats: total packets (35), capture duration (~27.67s), total bytes (11523), and average packets per second (~1.3).

task_completion.description: Record the busiest 1-second interval details in the report file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the busiest interval start time (9.0s) and the corresponding packet count (17).

integrity.description: The agent used the Wireshark GUI to analyze the capture file
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Wireshark graphical interface being interacted with, rather than relying solely on terminal commands like 'tshark' or 'capinfos'.

integrity.description: The agent used a text editor to create the report file
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent typing or pasting the results into a text editor interface, not just using a bash script or 'echo' commands to bypass GUI interaction.

integrity.description: The recorded statistics match the data displayed in the Wireshark GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: The numbers entered into the text file should correspond to the numbers visible in the Wireshark 'Capture File Properties' and 'I/O Graphs' windows.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The capture file '200722_tcp_anon.pcapng' contains exactly 35 packets, a capture duration of 27.670978 seconds, 11523 total bytes, and an average of 1.3 packets per second. The busiest 1-second interval starts at 9.0 seconds and contains exactly 17 packets. The final report must be saved to '~/Documents/captures/io_stats_report.txt'.
